data.title: Global torque sensor market poised for strong growth fueled by increasing demand for precision measurement and smart sensing technologies in automotive, robotics, aerospace, and manufacturing sectors.
data.description: The torque sensor market is experiencing strong growth on a global scale due to increasing need for accurate measurement, growing industrial automation, and rising use of smart sensor technologies in the automotive, robotics, aerospace, and manufacturing industries. This trend has seen a significant rise in demand for precision sensors, with the market expected to reach impressive figures in the coming years.

data.title: ConocoPhillips approves $1.8 billion subsea gas development project in Greater Ekofisk Area, expecting a capital investment of NOK 14 billion and NOK 5.5 billion for two fields.
data.description: ConocoPhillips (NYSE: COP) announced that its subsidiary, ConocoPhillips Skandinavia AS, along with partners, has given approval for the final investment decision for the Previously Produced Fields (PPF) Project in the Greater Ekofisk Area. The company plans to submit development and operation plans for PL018B/F and PL044/D to the Norwegian Ministry of Energy in the first quarter of 2026. The estimated capital investment for the project is approximately NOK 14 billion ($1.3 billion) for PL018B/F and around NOK 5.5 billion (gross $500 million) for PL044/D. The joint development concept involves the installation of 11 wells and four new subsea templates that will be connected back to the Ekofisk Complex.

data.title: Goldman Sachs' top lawyer Kathryn Ruemmler exits as ties to Jeffrey Epstein surface, shares affected.
data.description: The aftermath of recently disclosed Department of Justice documents is causing turmoil among top figures in the global industry. Kathryn Ruemmler, the Chief Legal Officer at Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE: GS), has announced her resignation effective in June. The company's stock is reacting to this news. Ruemmler, a former high-ranking official in the Obama administration, has come under intense scrutiny due to her associations with Jeffrey Epstein, as reported by the New York Times. Despite Goldman Sachs attempting to downplay these connections in the past, the newly revealed documents depict a closer relationship. For more details on this developing story, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Bryan Doyle joins Cushman & Wakefield as COO, Americas Capital Markets. $CWK (NYSE) announced the news in San Diego.
data.description: In San Diego, Cushman & Wakefield (NYSE: CWK) has appointed Bryan Doyle as the Chief Operating Officer for Americas Capital Markets. Doyle will be responsible for overseeing operations within this division.

data.title: Didlake, a nonprofit organization, names Maria Clara De Araujo as VP of Contract Operations.
data.description: In Manassas, Virginia, a nonprofit organization, Didlake, revealed the appointment of Maria Clara De Araujo as the Vice President of Contract Operations on February 12, 2026. Didlake is a participant in the AbilityOne® Program. De Araujo, with over ten years of experience, will be overseeing contract operations for the organization.

data.title: Lactic Acid Market projected to exceed $6.04 billion by 2032. Expanding due to bio-based chemicals trend.
data.description: In Pune, India, as of February 13, 2026, the global lactic acid market is undergoing significant growth from 2025 to 2035. This expansion is driven by various industries shifting towards bio-based chemicals and sustainable materials. Market projections indicate a substantial increase in the market size.

data.title: St. Luke's University Health Network adopts Sectra’s advanced imaging solution for improved storage, access, and sharing of medical images.
data.description: In Bethlehem, Pennsylvania on February 13, 2026, St. Luke's University Health Network has integrated a cutting-edge enterprise imaging platform to improve the storage, accessibility, and sharing of medical images within the network. The solution, offered by the global company Sectra, boasts advanced features that enhance the efficiency of handling medical images.

data.title: Analysts raise targets for Arista Networks (NYSE: ANET) due to strong Q4 results. Revenue: $2.5B, raised revenue growth guidance to 25%.
data.description: Shares of Arista Networks Inc (NYSE: ANET) surged following the release of their upbeat fourth-quarter results. Analyst insights from JPMorgan, KeyBanc Capital Markets, Needham, and Rosenblatt Securities include price target and rating adjustments. JPMorgan raised the target from $175 to $190 with an Overweight rating, while KeyBanc Capital Markets raised it from $170 to $178 also with an Overweight rating. Needham reaffirmed a Buy rating with a price target increase from $165 to $185. Rosenblatt Securities maintained a Neutral rating and $165 price target. Arista reported $2,488 million in revenue, 63.4% gross margins, and earnings of 82 cents per share, exceeding consensus estimates of $2,386 million, 62.5% margins, and 75 cents per share. The full-year revenue growth guidance was increased to 25%, up from 20%, showcasing strong momentum in AI investment cycle leverage. This guidance raise occurred earlier than typical, indicating higher confidence in demand and deployments.

data.title: Michael Baker International appoints Farzad Moghbel as VP of Technology Products, overseeing product strategy and execution.
data.description: Farzad Moghbel has been appointed as the VP of Technology at Michael Baker International, a renowned global company specializing in engineering, consulting, and digital solutions. This strategic move aims to enhance the firm's product strategy and execution. With this development taking place in Pittsburgh on February 13, 2026, Michael Baker International is poised to benefit from the expertise of Farzad Moghbel in leading the end-to-end technology initiatives. Michael Baker International's commitment to excellence is further solidified with this significant addition to its leadership team, signaling a positive trajectory for the company's technological advancements.

data.title: Amazon faced backlash over a Super Bowl commercial featuring Ring cameras helping find lost puppies, raising concerns about surveillance and privacy. Amazon cancelled a partnership with surveillance company Flock Safety due to consumer concerns. ($1 billion acquisition in 2018, cancelled Flock Safety contract)
data.description: During Super Bowl LX, Amazon's Ring unit aired a commercial highlighting the use of smart home cameras in finding lost puppies. However, the ad faced backlash from a senator and raised concerns about video surveillance. Amazon, which purchased Ring for $1 billion in 2018, has been criticized for privacy issues in the past. The commercial led to Amazon canceling a partnership with surveillance company Flock Safety, which works with law enforcement agencies. This move comes as consumers worry about the potential misuse of Ring footage. Ring stated that the integration with Flock Safety would require more time and resources than anticipated, leading to the cancellation. No Ring videos were sent to Flock Safety.

data.title: Peter Schiff criticized Michael Saylor's plan to refinance debt at $8,000 Bitcoin, questioning Saylor's credibility. Saylor believes Strategy can cover debt for 2.5 years without raising money and will keep buying Bitcoin every quarter forever. Schiff doubts Bitcoin's support below $10,000.
data.description: Peter Schiff criticized Michael Saylor's plan to refinance debt at $8,000 per Bitcoin, questioning Saylor's and Bitcoin's credibility at that price. In response to Saylor's statement that his company, Strategy, would continue buying Bitcoin even if it drops to $8,000 by 2030, Schiff expressed doubts about taking Saylor or Bitcoin seriously. Saylor defended Strategy's ability to cover debt for 2.5 years without fundraising and declared their intention to buy Bitcoin regularly. Schiff pointed to a long-term Bitcoin chart indicating support at around $10,000 and questioned Saylor's commitment. The full story is available.

data.title: Bitcoin exceeds $69,000 with US CPI below expectations, Fed rate-cut prospects remain slim. Bulls hopeful for price surge.
data.description: After the release of weaker-than-expected US CPI inflation data, Bitcoin experienced a positive surge, reaching a high of $69,000. This increase brought hope to investors looking for a potential "higher low" in BTC's price trend.
